CID and Hashing

CID and Hashing

Overview

CID (Content Identifier) is a self-describing content address that combines a hash function, hash digest, and codec. It enables content-addressed storage and verification.

CID Structure

CIDv1: z<multibase><multicodec><multihash>

Example: zbafy2bzaced4ueelaegfs5fq4a3fvh2ijmmq7xjlmakivezbsxyhynaiksqq

z       - multibase (base32)
bafy    - multicodec (dag-cbor)
2bzaced... - multihash (sha256 hash)

Multihash Format

<hash-function-code><digest-length><digest>

Example: 12 20 <32-byte-sha256-hash>

12      - SHA-256 code
20      - 32 bytes (hex)
<hash>  - 32-byte SHA-256 digest

CID Class

@interface CID : NSObject

// Creating CIDs
+ (nullable NSString *)createCIDv1:(NSData *)hash
                             codec:(NSString *)codec
                             error:(NSError **)error;

+ (nullable NSString *)createCIDv0:(NSData *)hash error:(NSError **)error;

// Parsing CIDs
+ (nullable NSDictionary *)parseCID:(NSString *)cid error:(NSError **)error;

// Validation
+ (BOOL)isValidCID:(NSString *)cid error:(NSError **)error;

// Conversion
+ (nullable NSString *)convertCIDv0toV1:(NSString *)cidv0 error:(NSError **)error;

@end

Hashing

SHA-256 Hashing

// 1. Encode record to CBOR
NSData *cborData = [ATProtoCBORSerialization encodeRecord:record error:&error];

// 2. Hash with SHA-256
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:cborData];

// 3. Create CID
NSString *cid = [CID createCIDv1:hash codec:@"dag-cbor" error:&error];

Creating CIDs

For Records

// 1. Encode record
NSDictionary *post = @{
    @"text": @"Hello!",
    @"createdAt": @"2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
};

NSData *cborData = [ATProtoCBORSerialization encodeRecord:post error:&error];

// 2. Hash
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:cborData];

// 3. Create CID
NSString *recordCid = [CID createCIDv1:hash codec:@"dag-cbor" error:&error];

// 4. Store record with CID
[recordService putRecord:@"app.bsky.feed.post"
                   rkey:@"abc123"
                  value:post
                 forDid:userDid
               actorDid:userDid
         validationMode:PDSValidationModeOptimistic
                  error:&error];

For Blocks

// 1. Encode block (CBOR or JSON)
NSData *blockData = [ATProtoCBORSerialization encodeObject:blockObject error:&error];

// 2. Hash
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:blockData];

// 3. Create CID
NSString *blockCid = [CID createCIDv1:hash codec:@"dag-cbor" error:&error];

// 4. Store block
[blobService storeBlock:blockData forCID:blockCid];

Parsing CIDs

// 1. Parse CID
NSError *error = nil;
NSDictionary *cidInfo = [CID parseCID:@"bafy2bzaced..." error:&error];

if (cidInfo) {
    NSString *version = cidInfo[@"version"];      // "1"
    NSString *codec = cidInfo[@"codec"];          // "dag-cbor"
    NSString *hashFunction = cidInfo[@"hash"];    // "sha256"
    NSData *digest = cidInfo[@"digest"];          // hash bytes
}

Validation

Verifying Content

// 1. Get content by CID
NSData *content = [blobService getBlob:cidData forDid:userDid error:&error];

// 2. Hash content
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:content];

// 3. Parse CID to get expected hash
NSDictionary *cidInfo = [CID parseCID:cidString error:&error];
NSData *expectedHash = cidInfo[@"digest"];

// 4. Verify
if ([hash isEqualToData:expectedHash]) {
    // Content matches CID
} else {
    // Content corrupted or wrong
}

CIDv0 vs CIDv1

CIDv0 (Legacy)

Qm... (base58 encoded)
- Only supports dag-pb codec
- Shorter but less flexible

CIDv1 (Current)

bafy... (base32 encoded)
- Supports multiple codecs
- Self-describing
- Recommended for new code

Conversion

// Convert v0 to v1
NSError *error = nil;
NSString *cidv1 = [CID convertCIDv0toV1:@"Qm..." error:&error];

// Result: bafy...

Best Practices

  1. CID Generation
  2. Use CIDv1 for new content
  3. Use SHA-256 for hashing
  4. Use dag-cbor for records

  5. Verify CID matches content

  6. Storage

  7. Store content by CID
  8. Deduplicate by CID
  9. Verify on retrieval

  10. Cache CID lookups

  11. Transmission

  12. Include CID with content
  13. Verify CID on receipt
  14. Use CID for integrity checks

  15. Support both v0 and v1

  16. Performance

  17. Cache hash computations
  18. Batch CID generation
  19. Use streaming for large content
  20. Monitor hash time

Common Patterns

Storing a Record with CID

// 1. Create record
NSDictionary *post = @{
    @"text": @"Hello!",
    @"createdAt": @"2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
};

// 2. Encode and hash
NSData *cborData = [ATProtoCBORSerialization encodeRecord:post error:&error];
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:cborData];
NSString *cid = [CID createCIDv1:hash codec:@"dag-cbor" error:&error];

// 3. Store record
[recordService putRecord:@"app.bsky.feed.post"
                   rkey:@"abc123"
                  value:post
                 forDid:userDid
               actorDid:userDid
         validationMode:PDSValidationModeOptimistic
                  error:&error];

// 4. Return CID to client
response[@"uri"] = [NSString stringWithFormat:@"at://%@/app.bsky.feed.post/abc123", userDid];
response[@"cid"] = cid;

Verifying Content Integrity

// 1. Get content
NSData *content = [blobService getBlob:cidData forDid:userDid error:&error];

// 2. Verify CID
NSData *hash = [CryptoUtils sha256:content];
NSDictionary *cidInfo = [CID parseCID:cidString error:&error];

if (![hash isEqualToData:cidInfo[@"digest"]]) {
    NSLog(@"Content corrupted!");
    return NO;
}

return YES;

See Also

  • CBOR Serialization
  • CAR Format
  • Blob Storage
